narration: You crouch down to the sweeper's level.
response: The blue sensor swivels to face you. The unit emits a sequence of chirps — two short, one long — and its brush spins briefly. It seems pleased to have company. A small display on its back flickers: CUSTODIAL UNIT 7. STATUS: OPERATIONAL. YEARS OF SERVICE: 1,247.

The sweeper unit rocks back and forth urgently. Its display scrolls: PARTICULATE LEVELS: UNACCEPTABLE. It spins in a tight circle, brush whirring, then stops and tilts its sensor up at you as if to say: you see what I'm dealing with here?

narration: You touch the ribbon on its antenna gently.
response: The unit goes very still. Its sensor dims briefly, then brightens. A single low tone — almost like a purr. The display reads: GIFT. CUSTODIAN YARA. YEAR 814. DO NOT REMOVE. It seems to regard the ribbon as very important.
The display flickers: CUSTODIAL UNIT 7. Then, after a pause, it adds: SOME CALLED ME SEVEN. Another pause. SOME CALLED ME PEST. The brush spins cheerfully at the second one.

The sweeper rolls to a spot near the wall and nudges it with its brush. Its display reads: THIS SECTION: 847 YEARS CONTINUOUS MAINTENANCE. FLOOR CONDITION: ADEQUATE. It pauses, then adds: NOT GREAT. The sensor dims slightly, as if embarrassed by the admission.

The sweeper unit spins excitedly, its brush revving. Then it stops, sensor dimming. The display reads: UNAUTHORIZED PERSONNEL MAY NOT OPERATE CUSTODIAL EQUIPMENT. It nudges against your foot apologetically. Then adds: BUT THANK YOU.

The sweeper goes still for a long moment. Its display cycles through fragments: CUSTODIAN YARA. DECK C MAINTENANCE LEAD. YEARS 790-846. Then simply: KIND. The sensor dims and brightens slowly, rhythmically. After a while it adds: YARA TALKED TO SEVEN. MOST DO NOT.

The display scrolls: ACTIVE POPULATION DECREASED. HIBERNATION EVENT YEAR 1241. ALL CUSTODIANS ENTERED SLEEP CYCLE. SEVEN CONTINUES ASSIGNED DUTIES. It pauses, then adds: IT IS QUIET NOW. The sensor dims. The brush spins once, slowly, like a sigh.

The sweeper chirps twice — the same greeting pattern — and resumes its slow circuit of the junction floor. Its brush hums contentedly. The display briefly reads: GOODBYE. Then returns to: PARTICULATE LEVELS: UNACCEPTABLE.
